summary: Stage 2 adversarial QC re-opened every load-bearing Florida citation on its official source: § 1010.04(4)(a), § 1006.21(5), § 1006.27, § 287.057(13)-(14), § 287.012 (definition of 'agency'), Fla. Admin. Code R. 6A-1.012(7)/(6)/(12)(d)/(12)(e) (via the elaws.us mirror), R. 6A-3.0171 (elaws.us mirror confirms '101 calendar days'), and both AG opinions (Duval Apr. 25, 2001 and the board-term-exceeding-members opinion) all re-verified verbatim. The principal Stage 1 audit finding is confirmed in full: live 2024/2025 text of § 1006.22 on flsenate.gov no longer carries the (1)(a) physically-handicapped/specialized-program language or the (6) common-carrier text the source-ledger quotes; the history note explicitly shows 's. 17, ch. 2023-16'; the deliverable's special-ed and Notes block is anchored to superseded statutory text under a 2022 URL paired with a '(2024)' label. Fla. Stat. § 1006.22 was amended by ch. 2023-16; subsection (1) now reads only 'District school boards may authorize the transportation of students in privately owned motor vehicles on a case-by-case basis,' (5) is the common-carrier provision, (6) addresses adult students. The FLDOE Appendix A docushare PDF returns as binary unparsed (WebFetch) and Firecrawl now reports 'free credits exhausted' — confirmed unreachable on this pass. The FLDOE TAP FY 2001-13 PDF returns 403 (WebFetch); Firecrawl unavailable — confirmed unreachable. The Broward fortlauderdale.gov showdocument URL returns 403 (WebFetch); Firecrawl unavailable — confirmed unreachable. Independent web search corroborates the underlying ITB-18-014R but indicates an April 3, 2017 release (not 'approved June 13, 2017') and 'three (3) vendors awarded' on the original release with an additional release Aug. 24, 2017 — the public search snippet does not directly contradict the deliverable's 'three renewal periods' source-ledger phrasing, but the research-findings text 'three one-year renewal options' and the 'approved June 13, 2017' date are not independently corroborated. Overall verdict: Minor — all load-bearing legal claims (max term, rebid, renewal, their primary citations) re-verified on official sources; supporting/Notes content has currency (§ 1006.22) and Broward-detail defects to remediate.

citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Live text re-verified verbatim: 'The State Board of Education may, by rule, provide for alternative procedures for school districts and Florida College System institutions for bidding or purchasing in cases in which the character of the item requested renders competitive bidding impractical.' This is the delegation under which Rule 6A-1.012 was adopted. Supports the deliverable's statutory-citation field and the 'No statutory maximum' / 'Required' findings.

Live text re-verified verbatim: 'Contiguous school districts shall make provisions for reciprocal policies and agreements for contracts for school bus transportation services, inspections, and screening requirements for public schools and public charter schools.' Supports the charter-school Notes paragraph.

citationVerifications.citation: Fla. Stat. § 1006.22 (cited as 2024; URL is the 2022 edition)
citationVerifications.claimedTierOrUrl: Tier 1 codified statute — https://www.flsenate.gov/laws/statutes/2022/1006.22 (ledger label says '§ 1006.22 (2024)' and asserts 'substantively unchanged in 2024-2025')
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: The 2022 URL itself re-opens and the 2022 edition does contain (1)(a) physically-handicapped/isolated and 'comprehensive contract for a specialized educational program' language and a (6) common-carrier provision. HOWEVER, the live 2024 edition at https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2024/1006.22 has been amended by ch. 2023-16, s. 17 (history note re-verified verbatim) and now reads: subsection (1) — 'District school boards may authorize the transportation of students in privately owned motor vehicles on a case-by-case basis'; subsection (5) — common-carrier athletic/school-function provision; subsection (6) — 'Transportation for adult students may be provided by any appropriate means as authorized by the district school board when the transportation is accepted as a responsibility by the district school board as provided in s. 1006.21.' The ledger's 'substantively unchanged in 2024-2025' assertion is contradicted by the live 2024 text. Citation/edition MISMATCH and supersession of the quoted subsections — non-load-bearing for max-term/rebid/renewal/primary-citation but affects the special-ed analysis and Notes block.

Live text re-verified verbatim: (1) voluntary bus-bid pooling by department; (2) district may purchase buses through department and resell to contractor. No term cap, renewal mechanism, or competitive-bidding rule. History note re-verified — last amended s. 19, ch. 2024-230. Supports the deliverable's 'silence' findings on max term and renewal mechanics.

(13) re-verified: 'Extension of a contract for commodities or contractual services must be in writing for a period not to exceed 6 months... There may be only one extension of a contract...' (14) re-verified: 'Contracts for commodities or contractual services may be renewed for a period that may not exceed 3 years or the term of the original contract, whichever is longer.' Subject throughout is 'agencies' as defined in § 287.012. Supports the deliverable's holding that the Ch. 287 renewal cap does not bind district school boards.

Definition re-verified verbatim: '"Agency" means any of the various state officers, departments, boards, commissions, divisions, bureaus, and councils and any other unit of organization, however designated, of the executive branch of state government.' Excludes university/college boards of trustees and state universities/colleges; does not include district school boards. Supports the deliverable's holding that § 287.057 renewal/extension caps do not bind district school boards.

citationVerifications.claimedTierOrUrl: Tier 2 state administrative code — https://flrules.elaws.us/fac/6a-1.012/ (third-party mirror; flrules.org official gateway renders metadata only)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Live text on elaws.us mirror re-verified verbatim: (7) 'Except as authorized by law or rule, competitive solicitations shall be requested from three (3) or more sources for any authorized commodities or contractual services exceeding $50,000.'; (6) piggyback authority; (12)(d) sole-source with 7-business-day public posting; (12)(e) emergency procurement on superintendent's written finding. Last amended Feb. 25, 2009. Methodology note acknowledged — this is the elaws.us mirror, not the Florida Department of State publication itself.

citationVerifications.citation: Fla. Admin. Code R. 6A-3.0171 (Responsibilities of School Districts for Student Transportation)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Gateway resolves and confirms rule title and Dec. 21, 2025 effective date. Gateway does not render full rule text inline. Stage 2 additionally pulled the elaws.us mirror (https://flrules.elaws.us/fac/6a-3.0171/) and confirmed verbatim: 'Inspections of school buses shall be conducted at an interval not to exceed 101 calendar days between inspections.' This resolves the prior Stage 1 audit concern that '101 calendar days' could not be inline-corroborated. The rule contains no contract-term, renewal, or bidding provision. Generic-portal-not-pinpoint flag retained for the flrules.org URL; elaws.us provides pinpoint text.

citationVerifications.citation: Fla. AG Informal Op. (Duval County School Board, Apr. 25, 2001)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Live page re-verified verbatim: 'Accordingly, in light of the above, I am of the opinion that the school board is required to adopt policies providing for the competitive bidding of such contracts as the school bus transportation service contracts.' URL slug misspells 'transportation' but that is the AG portal's own slug — not a deliverable defect.

citationVerifications.citation: Fla. AG Opinion — school board contract term exceeding members' terms
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: Conclusion re-verified verbatim: 'Accordingly, absent a constitutional or statutory prohibition against such action, the school board has the authority to enter into contracts serving a school purpose for terms in excess of the terms of the board's members.' Supports the no-statutory-maximum finding.

citationVerifications.citation: FLDOE 'Appendix A - Statutes and Rules for Student Transportation'
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: WebFetch returned binary PDF unparsed inline (cannot extract text); Firecrawl now returns 'free unauthenticated credits exhausted — re-authorization required.' Could not re-read verbatim on this pass. Deliverable acknowledges it as confirmatory only; no load-bearing claim affected. Confirmed unreachable on Stage 2 (matches Stage 1).

citationVerifications.citation: FLDOE Technical Assistance Paper FY 2001-13 (Transportation and Students with Disabilities)
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: HTTP 403 on WebFetch (with one retry); Firecrawl unavailable on this pass. Could not re-verify verbatim. The conclusion (IEP teams decide whether transportation is a related service) is consistent with general public FLDOE ESE practice but the documentary anchor is unreachable. No load-bearing claim affected. Confirmed unreachable on Stage 2 (matches Stage 1).

citationVerifications.citation: School Board of Broward County, Florida — School-bus transportation services ITB / renewal documentation
citationVerifications.sourceSaysVsClaim: HTTP 403 on WebFetch (with retry); Firecrawl unavailable. Independent web search corroborates the document exists at the fortlauderdale.gov URL (snippet title 'Status; REVISED THE SCHOOL BOARD OF BROWARD COUNTY, FLORIDA') and identifies the underlying ITB as ITB-18-014R, released April 3, 2017 with an additional release Aug. 24, 2017. The deliverable's 'approved June 13, 2017' and 'three one-year renewal options' specifics are not corroborated by the public search; the source-ledger phrasing 'three renewal periods' likewise cannot be reconciled to the inaccessible PDF on this pass. Confirmed unreachable on Stage 2 (matches Stage 1).

mismatches: source-ledger.md line 161 attaches the 2022 URL (https://www.flsenate.gov/laws/statutes/2022/1006.22) to a citation labeled 'Fla. Stat. § 1006.22 (2024)' and asserts the text is 'substantively unchanged in 2024-2025'; the live 2024 edition is materially different — § 1006.22 was amended by ch. 2023-16, s. 17 (history note re-verified) so the quoted (1)(a) physically-handicapped/specialized-program language and the (6) common-carrier text are no longer in those subsections.

research-findings.md line 173 ('Special-education differences') quotes § 1006.22(1)(a) and § 1006.22(6) verbatim from the 2022 text; both quotations no longer appear in those positions in the 2024/2025 official edition.

research-findings.md line 35 (Florida row) and line 175 (Notes) reference Rule 6A-3.0171's 2025 amendment 'inspection frequency (quarterly, max 101 calendar days).' Stage 2 elaws.us mirror confirms the '101 calendar days' phrase verbatim; the flrules.org gateway URL cited in the source-ledger does not render this inline (gateway is metadata-only). This is a generic-portal-not-pinpoint flag for the cited URL, not a substantive error.

research-findings.md line 35 (Florida row, District Variance) claims 'School Board of Broward County ITB (approved June 13, 2017) structured with three one-year renewal options exercisable by mutual agreement and Board approval.' Source-ledger line 171 phrases this as 'three renewal periods' with a final renewal 7/1/2021-6/30/2022. Independent web search indicates the underlying ITB-18-014R was released April 3, 2017 with additional release Aug. 24, 2017 (not 'approved June 13, 2017'); the fortlauderdale.gov source URL is unreachable so the verbatim 'three renewal periods' phrasing cannot be reconciled on this pass.

verification-notes.md line 106 ('Current-Law Check') addresses Chapter 1006 Subpart E, Chapter 1010 Part I, and Chapter 287 in the aggregate ('no 2025-2026 session-law amendments... affect transportation-contract terms/renewal/bidding') but does not separately flag the ch. 2023-16, s. 17 amendment to § 1006.22 — the amendment that supersedes the 2022 text quoted in the source-ledger and research-findings.

challenge-matrix.md row for 'Currentness' (line 105) addresses § 1006.27 (ch. 2024-230) and Rule 6A-3.0171 (Dec. 21, 2025) but does not include § 1006.22 (ch. 2023-16, s. 17) — the very amendment that bears on the special-ed and Notes content.

issues.evidence: Live 2024 edition of Fla. Stat. § 1006.22 on flsenate.gov (https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2024/1006.22) re-verified: subsection (1) reads only 'District school boards may authorize the transportation of students in privately owned motor vehicles on a case-by-case basis'; subsection (5) is the common-carrier provision; subsection (6) addresses adult students. History note re-verified: 'History.—s. 296, ch. 2002-387; s. 14, ch. 2006-301; s. 22, ch. 2019-119; s. 38, ch. 2022-154; s. 17, ch. 2023-16.' source-ledger line 161 attaches the 2022 URL to a '(2024)' label and asserts 'substantively unchanged in 2024-2025' — contradicted by the live current text. research-findings line 173 anchors the entire special-education paragraph and the 'comprehensive contract for a specialized educational program' language to the superseded (1)(a) text. Special-ed conclusion itself (no separate procurement regime; IEP team decides whether transportation is a related service) is independently supportable, but its statutory anchor is wrong as a matter of currentness.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Re-anchor every special-ed and Notes quotation to the current § 1006.22 text (2024 edition) at the correct subsection numbers, OR explicitly label the 2022 wording as 'prior text, superseded by ch. 2023-16, s. 17' if retention serves a historical-context purpose. Update the source-ledger URL to https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2024/1006.22 and remove the 'substantively unchanged in 2024-2025' assertion.

issues.evidence: source-ledger line 161 attaches https://www.flsenate.gov/laws/statutes/2022/1006.22 (2022 edition path) to a citation labeled 'Fla. Stat. § 1006.22 (2024).' Label says 2024; URL points to 2022. Citation/edition mismatch on its face — flagged by Stage 1 audit and independently re-verified on Stage 2.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Replace the URL with the 2024 edition (https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2024/1006.22) and reconcile the quoted text to whatever the 2024 edition actually says, or change the citation label to '(2022)' and disclose the supersession by ch. 2023-16, s. 17.

issues.evidence: https://www.fortlauderdale.gov/home/showdocument?id=60840&t=637606456961884107 returned HTTP 403 on WebFetch (with retry); Firecrawl now reports 'free unauthenticated credits exhausted — re-authorization required' on this pass. The Broward fortlauderdale.gov source URL could not be re-opened directly on either Stage 1 or Stage 2. Independent web search corroborates the document exists (search snippet title 'Status; REVISED THE SCHOOL BOARD OF BROWARD COUNTY, FLORIDA').
issues.recommendedRemediation: Replace the showdocument querystring URL with a durable equivalent (e.g., http://www.broward.k12.fl.us/supply/agenda/ITB_18-014R_SchoolBusTransportationServices.pdf — surfaced by independent search) or include both URLs as redundant citations. Add a backup district-policy citation.

issues.evidence: research-findings line 35 claims 'School Board of Broward County ITB (approved June 13, 2017) structured with three one-year renewal options exercisable by mutual agreement and Board approval.' Independent web-search snippets from broward.k12.fl.us / browardschools.com indicate ITB-18-014R was released April 3, 2017 with an additional release Aug. 24, 2017 and three vendors awarded on the original release; the public search snippets do not corroborate 'June 13, 2017' as an approval date and do not directly state 'three one-year renewal options.' source-ledger line 171 uses the slightly different phrasing 'three renewal periods'; the fortlauderdale.gov PDF could not be re-opened to reconcile the verbatim claim. Stage 1 audit further notes Stage-1 research summaries identifying two (not three) one-year renewal options for ITB-18-014R.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Re-open the fortlauderdale.gov PDF on an authenticated session (or use the broward.k12.fl.us URL surfaced by independent search) and reconcile the verbatim claim. If the document is a city-side contract referencing a separate Broward agenda, cite the Broward agenda item directly for the renewal-option count and approval date. Correct 'June 13, 2017' and the renewal-option count to whatever the source actually says.

issues.evidence: source-ledger line 165 cites https://flrules.elaws.us/fac/6a-1.012/ as the primary-text source. The flrules.org official gateway returned metadata only on Stage 1 (re-verified on Stage 2). elaws.us is a consistently maintained third-party mirror, not the Florida Department of State publication itself. Methodology note in verification-notes acknowledges the substitution.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Add the official flrules.org gateway URL alongside the elaws.us mirror, with a methodology note that the gateway renders metadata only and links a downloadable rule text. Link the rule-text file directly when available.

issues.evidence: verification-notes line 106 ('Current-Law Check') addresses Chapter 1006 Subpart E, Chapter 1010 Part I, and Chapter 287 in the aggregate but does not flag the ch. 2023-16, s. 17 amendment to § 1006.22 — the very amendment that supersedes the 2022 text quoted in the source-ledger and research-findings. challenge-matrix line 105 (currentness row) also does not enumerate the § 1006.22 amendment.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Add a verification-notes line explicitly addressing ch. 2023-16, s. 17 and its impact on the special-ed / Notes language; add a corresponding challenge-matrix row for the currentness of the special-ed finding.

issues.field: statutoryCitation (FLDOE Appendix A & TAP FY 2001-13 unreachability)
issues.evidence: FLDOE Appendix A docushare PDF returned binary unparsed on WebFetch; FLDOE TAP FY 2001-13 returned HTTP 403 on WebFetch (with retry); Firecrawl unavailable on Stage 2 (free-credits exhausted). Both citations therefore unreachable on Stage 1 and Stage 2. Source-ledger lines 169-170 retain the dead-on-arrival URLs. Pack acknowledges Appendix A as 'binary; treated as confirmatory only'; TAP is non-load-bearing.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Add a methodology note that these agency-hosted PDFs are host-side-dependent for retrievability; supply a fallback citation (e.g., an archived copy via Wayback or the FLDOE ESE webpage indexing the TAP) so a future re-verifier can resolve them. Do not silently retain the dead-on-arrival URLs.

issues.field: challenge-matrix coverage (McKinney-Vento / foster-care negative search visibility)
issues.evidence: Special-ed challenge row (challenge-matrix line 103) references 'searched for separate McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or alternative-transportation procurement statutes in Florida' but does not enumerate the official-statute citations searched. Stage 2 disconfirming searches did not surface any Florida statute creating a separate procurement regime for those populations — substantive finding holds, methodological visibility is the only gap.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Expand the special-ed challenge row to enumerate the specific Florida McKinney-Vento (e.g., Fla. Stat. § 1003.21 / federal Title VII-B linkage) and foster-care (e.g., Fla. Stat. § 1003.218) searches by hit/no-hit so the negative search path is visible.

issues.evidence: research-findings line 35 (Florida row) and line 175 (Notes) reference '101 calendar days' as Rule 6A-3.0171's 2025-amended inspection-frequency requirement. The cited URL (https://flrules.org/gateway/RuleNo.asp?title=Transportation&ID=6A-3.0171) is the official gateway — metadata only — and does not render '101 calendar days' inline. Stage 2 confirms the phrase verbatim on the elaws.us mirror (https://flrules.elaws.us/fac/6a-3.0171/). Stage 1 audit had flagged that '101 calendar days' could not be inline-corroborated on the cited URL.
issues.recommendedRemediation: Add the elaws.us mirror URL (https://flrules.elaws.us/fac/6a-3.0171/) alongside the flrules.org gateway URL in the source-ledger for Rule 6A-3.0171, or substitute the pinpoint source, so the '101 calendar days' quotation has a working pinpoint.
